candidates.name: Mailchimp Campaign Subject Line A/B Winner Predictor
candidates.pitch: Reads the A/B setup screen in Mailchimp and scores each subject variant using historical open-rate embeddings plus an LLM judge. Freemium: 10 predictions/month. Differentiator from warmup tools: focus
candidates.pain: Email marketers can't predict which Mailchimp subject line variant will win before sending, wasting send volume on losers.

candidates.name: Local Browser AI Long-Doc Summarizer
candidates.pitch: Uses Chrome's built-in Gemini Nano via the Prompt API to summarize articles and PDFs locally, never sending content off-device. Why now: Prompt API stabilized in Chrome 131+. Differentiator: Web Artic
candidates.pain: Long web docs and PDFs eat hours of reading time, but cloud AI summarizers leak proprietary content to vendors.

candidates.name: Calendly Round Robin Distribution Fairness Checker
candidates.pitch: Scrapes Calendly admin analytics over rolling 30-day windows and computes distribution skew versus tenure, ramp status, and timezone. Per-team pricing for SDR orgs. Niche but sticky because fairness c
candidates.pain: Sales managers suspect Calendly round-robin favors tenured reps but can't prove distribution is unfair without manual audit.

candidates.name: Gmail Receipt Auto-Categorizer for Expenses
candidates.pitch: Scans Gmail for receipt-like emails and extracts vendor, amount, date, and category via local LLM, exporting to CSV or QuickBooks IIF. Free = 25 receipts/month, paid unlimited with custom category rul
candidates.pain: Freelancers and small biz owners dig through Gmail monthly hunting for receipts to file into expense reports.

candidates.name: Vercel Deployment Preview Comment Collector
candidates.pitch: Scrapes GitHub PR comments tied to a Vercel deployment URL and overlays them on the preview page in a side panel. Buildable in ~3 weeks using Vercel deploy hooks + GitHub REST API. Existing Vercel-adj
candidates.pain: Reviewers leave scattered PR comments but the Vercel preview has no native way to thread them back to the deployment.

monetization_hint.model: Freemium → SaaS bridge (paid tier via external Stripe)

candidates.name: AWS Console Service Limit Headroom Calculator
candidates.pitch: Reads AWS Service Quotas via the console sidebar and overlays headroom % next to relevant resources. Free tier = 10 services tracked, paid = full coverage with alert routing. Why now: AWS Trusted Acce
candidates.pain: DevOps engineers discover they're at 95% of an AWS service limit only when a deployment fails or an API throttles.

monetization_hint.model: Freemium → SaaS bridge (paid tier via external Stripe)

candidates.name: Jira Sprint Scope Creep Detector
candidates.pitch: Tracks Jira sprint board changes in real time on the sprint page and computes scope drift % vs committed scope at planning time. Buildable in ~2 weeks. Strong cross-sell potential into a Linear/Asana
candidates.pain: Mid-sprint tickets silently balloon sprint scope, but standups never surface how much new work entered after planning.

monetization_hint.model: Stripe tip jar (Payment Links $3/$5/$10) + optional sponsor link

candidates.name: Shopify Customer Review Authenticity Scorer
candidates.pitch: Analyzes review metadata, language patterns, and reviewer history to score authenticity per product page. Side panel overlay on any Shopify storefront. Competitor gap: Yotpo/Judge.me focus on collecti
candidates.pain: Shopify merchants can't tell which product reviews are genuine versus paid/imported junk that tanks conversion trust.

candidates.name: Salesforce Opportunity Stakeholder Change Tracker
candidates.pitch: Monitors OpportunityContactRole history via Salesforce REST API and posts alerts when stakeholder composition shifts mid-cycle. Buildable in 2-3 weeks. Complementary to existing close-date slippage an
candidates.pain: AEs miss when a champion leaves a deal or a new economic buyer shows up, both critical signals for forecast calls.

candidates.name: Adobe Analytics Workspace Adoption Tracker
candidates.pitch: Pulls Adobe Analytics admin logs and ranks projects by usage, dwell time, and export frequency; surfaces a leaderboard while users are in the AA UI. Niche but high willingness-to-pay because it justif
candidates.pain: Analytics teams build dozens of Workspace projects nobody opens, but leadership has no data on actual report consumption.

monetization_hint.model: Freemium → SaaS bridge (paid tier via external Stripe)

candidates.name: DocuSign Envelope Field Anomaly Detector
candidates.pitch: Pre-flight check on the DocuSign envelope editor flags unusual field placement, missing required signers, and off-template usage. Buildable in ~3 weeks using the eSignature API. Per-seat pricing for l
candidates.pain: Legal teams catch misconfigured DocuSign envelopes (wrong signer, missing initials) only after sending, causing rework and embarrassment.

candidates.name: Linear Issue Acceptance Criteria Coverage Checker
candidates.pitch: Parses Linear issue descriptions and child issues, flags missing edge cases, ambiguity markers, and untestable criteria in a side panel. Sales motion: per-team pricing for product orgs. Builds on Line
candidates.pain: Linear issues ship to engineers with vague or missing acceptance criteria, leading to rework and 'that's not what I meant' PR ping-pong.
